title: Roast Chicken & Pepper with Mash
summary: Chicken and mash is an elite combination. This recipe uses up leftovers from our Roast Chicken & Peppers batch, so you're on the way to a speedy weeknight dinner you'll want on the table every week.

method.description: Peel the potatoes and chop into 2cm chunks. Add them into a saucepan full of salted water and place onto high heat. Boil for 15-20 mins, until cooked through. Drain, reserving 100ml of water.
Mash the potatoes. Place onto medium-low heat and stir through the butter and milk until incorporated. Season with salt and pepper.
Tip the juices into a frying pan and bring to the boil. Once boiling, turn the heat down to a simmer and cook the sauce out until slightly thickened, 3-5 mins.
Reheat the chicken, veggies and mash in the microwave until hot. If you don’t have a microwave, tip the chicken and veggies into a baking tray and roast at 220°C until hot, 5-10 mins.
Share the mash between bowls, top with the veggies and chicken. Drizzle over the sauce.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
